description.en: Deciduous small-to-medium tree (1–8 m), indigenous to Sri Lanka and South Asia. In Sri Lanka nelli is cultivated primarily in **home gardens**, not commercial farms — a ritual-and-medicine crop more than a market crop. Families prepare Nelli oil for hair and skin, brew it as tea, and use it in seasonal cleansing rites. Propagation: seed (7–10 years to flower), ring budding or veneer grafting (flower in 3–4 years).

scientific_name.quote: Phyllanthus emblica, also known as emblic, emblic myrobalan, myrobalan, nelikai, Indian gooseberry, Malacca tree, amloki or amla

duration_days.quote: Plants can be grown from seed, ring budding, or veneer grafting. Grafted trees begin flowering in 3–4 years, while seed-grown trees may take 7–10 years.

description.quote: In Sri Lanka, Nelli is cultivated in home gardens, not commercial farms—a reflection of its intimate, ritual-based role in daily life. Families prepare Nelli oil for hair and skin, brew it into teas, and offer it in seasonal cleansing rites.
